Name: IA-2: Empty Authorization header value is handled without server crash
Description: An empty auth header must return a safe client-error status, not a server error.

Name: AC-2: Every account is linked to an organisation
Description: Account records without an organisation cannot be audited for access scope.

Name: SC-28: Response body does not contain US Social Security Number patterns
Description: SSNs in API responses indicate a failure to protect PII at rest.

Name: IA-5: API key does not appear in any API response body
Description: An API key present in a response body indicates a credential leak in transit.

Name: SI-12: DELETE returns an appropriate status confirming removal
Description: A deletion must return a 2xx status confirming the retention policy was applied.

Name: SI-2: Response includes a Content-Type header
Description: Missing Content-Type enables MIME-sniffing attacks indicating unpatched behaviour.

Name: AC-3: Non-existent resource returns 404, not data
Description: Out-of-range resource IDs must return 404, not internal data.

Name: IR-6: Error responses have a consistent, parseable format
Description: Inconsistent error formats prevent automated incident reporting pipelines from parsing and escalating failures correctly.

Name: IR-6: 404 response contains enough information to file an incident report
Description: An incident report requires a status code and a parseable response body. A completely empty 404 cannot be reported accurately.

Name: AU-2: Failed request returns a loggable structured error
Description: Error responses must be structured so failures can be captured in an audit log.

Name: SC-8: API_BASE_URL uses HTTPS to enforce encrypted transmission
Description: Unencrypted transmission violates SC-8. All URLs must use the https scheme.

Name: AU-9: Audit record list is bounded and does not expose unlimited data
Description: The record list must return a bounded, finite set to prevent bulk audit export.

Name: AU-12: Generated record contains all required audit fields
Description: An audit record must carry userId, id, title, and body to be complete.

Name: SI-2: 404 response does not leak stack traces or file paths
Description: Error responses must not expose implementation details exploitable by attackers.

Name: SC-8: TLS handshake succeeds with strict certificate verification
Description: A successful TLS handshake with verify=True confirms valid certificate chain.

Name: AC-17: Remote API responds within 2000ms latency threshold
Description: Remote connections must respond within 2000ms to ensure availability.

Name: IA-2: Request with a malformed Authorization header is handled safely
Description: Malformed auth tokens must not cause 5xx errors or expose internal details.

Name: SI-10: API handles malicious input without crashing or leaking internals
Description: Every form of malicious or malformed input must be handled safely — no 5xx errors and no stack trace disclosure.

Name: IA-2: Repeated authentication failures do not expose internal error details
Description: Five consecutive bad-token requests must not trigger a 5xx or leak internals.

Name: AU-2: Create event produces an auditable record with assigned ID
Description: POST must return a 201 with an assigned ID so the creation event is auditable.

Name: SC-8: Response is served over HTTPS as confirmed by the effective URL
Description: The final URL after any redirects must still use the https scheme.

Name: AU-12: DELETE returns an audit-compatible 200 status
Description: A deletion event must return 200 so it can be captured in the audit log.

Name: AU-9: Audit records cannot be overwritten with arbitrary data
Description: PUT requests must not silently overwrite audit-critical fields with invalid data.

Name: SI-10: API handles malicious input without crashing or leaking internals
Description: Every form of malicious or malformed input must be handled safely — no 5xx errors and no stack trace disclosure.

Name: IA-5: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is present in the environment
Description: A missing API key means authenticator provisioning has failed.

Name: IR-5: Burst of rapid requests is handled consistently without errors
Description: Simulates an anomalous traffic spike. All 10 requests must return the same status code — no intermittent 5xx or inconsistent handling.

Name: AC-3: Response body does not expose sensitive keywords
Description: API responses must not leak passwords, secrets, or private keys.

Name: AU-9: All audit record IDs are valid positive integers
Description: Invalid record IDs undermine the integrity of the audit trail.

Name: SI-12: Record response does not include unexpected sensitive retention fields
Description: Responses must not expose internal retention metadata such as raw timestamps or checksums.

Name: IR-5: Unsupported HTTP method returns a client-error status
Description: An unsupported method (e.g. TRACE) on a resource simulates an anomalous probe. The server must return a 4xx, not crash with 5xx.

Name: SI-2: Server header does not expose version information
Description: Server version disclosure enables targeted exploitation of known CVEs.

Name: IA-5: API key meets minimum length requirement
Description: Short API keys are brute-forceable. Minimum 20 characters enforces key strength.

Name: SI-10: API handles malicious input without crashing or leaking internals
Description: Every form of malicious or malformed input must be handled safely — no 5xx errors and no stack trace disclosure.

Name: AC-3: Query filter restricts results to authorised records only
Description: A userId filter must return only posts belonging to that user.

Name: AC-2: Account list exposes only expected schema fields
Description: Every account record must contain id, name, email, and username.

Name: AC-17: BASE_URL uses HTTPS
Description: The application BASE_URL must use HTTPS to secure all remote sessions.

Name: AU-2: Successful resource fetch produces a traceable response
Description: A successful GET must return a response body with an identifiable record ID.

Name: SC-28: Response body does not contain plaintext credential patterns
Description: Plaintext passwords in API responses indicate data-at-rest protection failure.

Name: SI-10: API handles malicious input without crashing or leaking internals
Description: Every form of malicious or malformed input must be handled safely — no 5xx errors and no stack trace disclosure.

Name: IR-6: Successful API responses include identifiable metadata for incident correlation
Description: Responses must carry enough identifiable fields (id, userId) to correlate a successful event with an incident if it later becomes relevant.

Name: SI-12: Record list is bounded to prevent excessive data retention exposure
Description: An unbounded list response violates information retention scoping requirements.

Name: SI-10: API handles malicious input without crashing or leaking internals
Description: Every form of malicious or malformed input must be handled safely — no 5xx errors and no stack trace disclosure.

Name: AC-17: API_BASE_URL uses HTTPS
Description: All remote API access must use HTTPS to protect data in transit.

Name: IR-5: Request with wrong Content-Type is handled gracefully
Description: An anomalous Content-Type header simulates a misconfigured or malicious client. The server must respond safely without crashing.

Name: AC-2: All account IDs are unique
Description: Duplicate account IDs indicate a broken account management control.

Name: SC-28: Response body does not contain credit card number patterns
Description: PCI-DSS and SC-28 both prohibit unmasked credit card numbers in API responses.

Name: AU-12: POST generates a new record with a system-assigned ID
Description: Each created record must receive a unique system-assigned ID for audit generation.
